_sources.discrepancies: NCSL classifies Idaho as photo ID required. Idaho's primary is typically the third Tuesday in May; estimated 2026-05-19 for 2026. Idaho offers same-day voter registration at the polls. Idaho does not have a statewide early voting law but counties may offer absentee in-person voting. 2026 registration deadlines estimated from Idaho statute (25 days before election for online/mail); TODO verify exact dates.

earlyVoting.notes: Absentee in-person voting available at county clerk offices before Election Day. Hours vary by county. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
Absentee in-person voting available at county clerk offices. No uniform statewide early voting law — availability and hours vary by county. Contact your county clerk for details.

Idaho does not have a uniform statewide early voting law. Absentee in-person voting is available at county clerk offices before Election Day. Hours and start dates vary by county. Contact your county clerk for details.

acceptedIds: Idaho driver's license or identification card
ID card issued by a U.S. federal, state, or local government agency
Current student ID from an Idaho college or university (photo required)
License to carry concealed weapons issued by Idaho or any other state
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Idaho law prohibits use of a phone or electronic device to photograph a completed ballot in the voting booth. Counties may have additional rules. Contact your county clerk for specific polling place policies.
additionalRules: Idaho has a closed primary — voters must be registered with a party to vote in that party's primary. Unaffiliated voters may register with a party at the polls on primary day.
Same-day voter registration is available at the polls on Election Day — bring proof of Idaho residency.
Absentee voting is available to any voter without a required reason.

eligibility: Any registered Idaho voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse absentee)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Must be received by the county clerk by 5:00 p.m. on the Friday before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county clerk by 8:00 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the county clerk's office by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.
